steps.title: Identify the defining outcome
steps.content: The passage explains that slowed currents create “calmer waters where animals can take shelter.” The blank needs a word that characterizes this environment.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The word needs to mean calm and sheltered, since the passage says animals can "take shelter" in these slowed-current waters. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — ‘surprising’: unexpected or astonishing — the passage describes a predictable physical effect of slowed currents, not something unexpected. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘tranquil’: peaceful and still — exactly describes calmer, shelter-offering waters created by slower currents. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘imaginative’: creative or inventive — this is a human quality; it cannot describe a physical marine environment. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘dangerous’: hazardous — the opposite of what the passage says; the forests make the environment safer, not more dangerous. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
